statement.p:
Consider the expression \dfrac{x^a\cdot x^b}{x^c} where a\lt0, b\lt0\text{,} and c \gt0\text{.}
Are there values for a\text{,} b\text{,} and c so that the expression equals x^{7}\text{?} If so, fill in the blanks below with possible values for a\text{,} b\text{,} and c\text{.} If not, fill in the blanks below with the word none.
a = , b = , and c =
Are there values for a\text{,} b\text{,} and c so that the expression equals \frac{1}{x^{10}}\text{?} If so, fill in the blanks below with possible values for a\text{,} b\text{,} and c\text{.} If not, fill in the blanks below with the word none.
a = , b = , and c =

li.p: The problem states that \frac{x^a\cdot x^b}{x^c}=x^{7}\text{.} To simplify \frac{x^a\cdot x^b}{x^c}\text{,} we must add the exponents a and b\text{,} and then subtract the exponent c\text{.} Thus, a+b-c must equal 7. Since a and b are negative, a+b is a negative number. Then, we have to subtract c\text{,} which is a positive number. Thus, a+b-c will result in a negative number, but a+b-c must equal 7. Therefore, it is not possible to come up with values for a\text{,} b\text{,} and c that work.

First off, we must remember that \frac{1}{x^{10}}=x^{-10}\text{.} To simplify \frac{x^a\cdot x^b}{x^c}\text{,} we must add the exponents a and b\text{,} and then subtract the exponent c\text{.} Thus, a+b-c must equal -10. For example, 10+{-1}-2=-10\text{.}
